lyrics:
Every street a parking lot
Every driver lost in thought
Progress screams forget me not
Meter knows what time forgot

Ten thousand reasons to stress out
Just give yourself a quick timeout

Play the way the palm trees sway
Pray dismay will lose its gray
Make each day be a runway
Fly away without delay just for today
Try to be okay
Try to be okay
Try to be okay
Try to be okay

Jetway's broken, flight is late
First class asshole storm the gate
Frequent flyer miles inflate
Baggage knows its final fate

Ten thousand reasons to stress out
Just give yourself a quick timeout

Play the wave of palm trees sway
Pray dismay to lose its gray
Make each day be a runway
Fly away without delay just for today

Try to be okay when the weight begins to rise
And the truth feels wrapped in lies
Close your every eyes
Let the simple calm surprise

Play the wave of palm trees sway
Pray dismay to lose its gray
Make each day be a runway
Fly away without delay just for today
Try to be okay
Try to be okay
Try to be okay
Try to be okay
persona:
## PCBender

Rugged, wise, and deeply entrenched in the art of music-making, PCBender is more than just a musician—he’s a sonic craftsman. A guitar-slinging songwriter, producer, and storyteller, he embodies the soul of analog warmth, weaving together rich textures of classic rock, blues, and experimental soundscapes with a modern edge.

With a presence that carries the mystique of a seasoned explorer, PCBender is the Indiana Jones of music production—digging deep into the essence of sound, unearthing raw emotion, and shaping it into songs that resonate beyond time. His music is a fusion of grit and refinement, where worn-in guitar tones meet polished production, and every note feels like it was carved from experience.

A producer at heart, he doesn’t just create songs—he builds them. Each track is an expedition through layers of melody, distortion, and lyrical depth, reflecting both his technical expertise and his philosopher’s approach to songwriting. Whether delivering anthemic riffs, cinematic storytelling, or stripped-down acoustic wisdom, PCBender brings an authenticity that makes listeners feel like they’re sitting in the studio with him, watching music come to life.

As a key figure in Maricopa Records, PCBender bridges the gap between analog and AI-driven creativity, proving that the heart of a song is never about how it’s made, but why.

For those who seek music with depth, texture, and soul—PCBender is your guide.

[](https://open.spotify.com/artist/0aivrU155laeOcIoT4AhPo?si=pYdQp1J_S4y0vSFA4itgUg)[](https://music.apple.com/us/artist/pcbender/1793178641)[](https://music.youtube.com/channel/UCvNXkiIHQJM-uY4MT3gmKDw)![](https://www.maricoparecords.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/02/PCBender.png)
## Releases

impression.text: This track has a bright, multi-layered texture, creating an optimistic and confident emotional atmosphere driven by a funky synth bass, clean electric guitar, and punchy drums. The energy is consistently high and danceable, establishing a feel-good, anthemic groove with a polished, retro-pop sensibility. The production is exceptionally clean and spacious, allowing the lead vocal, rich harmonies, and various synth layers to occupy their own distinct space without feeling cluttered. A defining quality is the powerful chorus, where a memorable, soaring vocal melody and lush backing vocals create an infectious and uplifting hook that gives the song its core identity.

review.review_text: At 123 BPM in a clean G major, 'Try to Be Okay' moves the way its chorus preaches: with a kind of deliberate, sunny momentum that refuses to drag its feet. The hook lands hard, that soaring 'fly away without delay just for today' stacking harmonies in a way that recalls peak Tom Petty without borrowing his wardrobe, and the funky low end keeps things grounded when the song could easily float off into sentiment. The verses earn their place too, sketching airport frustration and traffic-choked streets with a specificity that makes the release of the chorus feel genuinely won. A highlight.
anchors_used: lyric: 'fly away without delay just for today'
verse imagery: parking lots, jetway, gate-storming passenger
